security_acceptance_criteria: MET: non-public posts indistinguishable from nonexistent on POST comment creation (status, message, envelope identical; no practical timing channel)
MET: public-post comment creation unchanged; locked-public-post 403 preserved with correct guard ordering
MET: all other comment routes (list, moderation, lock/unlock) byte-identical to merge-base
note_findings: Verifier pass-2 cites a docs/deferred-tasks.md entry for the resolvePostId->createComment TOCTOU that does not exist; security impact nil because the defense-in-depth write-time check is authoritative — planner should add the entry or record that no deferral is needed at the next planning cycle.

resolvePostId controller wiring is pinned only by simulated tests (private method not invoked); acceptable because the tested service-layer defense-in-depth guard independently preserves the invariant against a controller-layer revert.
Cosmetic 404-message variance between GET listComments and POST createComment routes; constant within each route, no oracle.

Pre-existing authenticated parentId/imageId distinguisher messages (400-level) on public posts allow second-order existence confirmation of comment/media UUIDs; out of scope, not worsened, candidate for a future data-minimization pass.
Known unchanged items not re-reported: slug/UUID namespace shadowing (fail-closed), public comment payload data-minimization (register line 17), 600-char source-contract window headroom.
test_sufficiency_summary: SUFFICIENT. 86/86 tests independently re-executed in this worktree (79 service + 7 controller).

Oracle-parity suite covers nonexistent/draft/unpublished/future-scheduled plus a message-identity test asserting all four produce the exact same NotFoundException class+message; locked-public-post regression pins ForbiddenException class and message; LessThanOrEqual FindOperator type now asserted (closes WA1 NOTE 1); fallback-wiring tests close WA1 NOTE 2 in simulated form.
documentation_sufficiency_summary: SUFFICIENT. docs/README.md corrected in all three locations with explicit existence-oracle-prevention language; createComment Swagger decorators now match implemented behavior and are guarded by three source-contract regression tests.
